free_text: Activation cross sections of proton and deuteron
induced nuclear reactions on holmium and erbium,
related to the production of 161Er and 160Er medical
isotopes
Er(p,x)161Er cross sections compiled separately
High purity metallic Ho and Er foils.
The stack1 contained 19 times of Al 10 um, In 116 um,
Al 99.2 um, V 8.41 um, Al 99.2 um, Ho 26.2 um,
Al 99.2 um foils. The stack was irradiated with
65 MeV, 35 nA protons for 1 h at 2BLGLVN.
The stack2 contained 13 times of Er 25 um, Co 50 um,
Al 10 um, In 50 um, Al 100 um foils. The stack was
irradiated with 70 MeV, 58 nA protons for 1 h at
2JPNTOH.
The covered energy range for erbium was 69.5-59.4 MeV.
Deuteron irradiations were done at 2BLGVUB and 2JPNTOH
at 21 MeV and 40 MeV on Er foils of 25 and 32 um thick
respectively using Ti and Al monitor foils.
Activity was determined with no chemical separation.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
The uncertainty of the energy scale was
estimated by taking into account the energy
uncertainty of the primary beam, the possible variation
in the target thickness and the effect of beam
straggling and cross checked through the re-measured
excitation function of monitor reaction.
No information on sources of uncertainties.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
